{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"readiness check timed out after 5m0s (ddx-334c0958)","fingerprint":"b2ddbdfa5d9901c0b19aa637cb850c3df14471da1bebc1447f28ce6c76cd4d2e","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"timeout","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.timeout","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"b2ddbdfa5d9901c0b19aa637cb850c3df14471da1bebc1447f28ce6c76cd4d2e"}

{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. Add Niflheim-evidence regression fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` that encode the four observed scenario families from this bead: empty review success, land/ref/index/evidence-staging failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. The fixtures must be self-contained and must not reference Niflheim paths as required inputs.","coverage":"Child 'Enforce execute-bead review evidence before success close' AC1 covers empty review success; child 'Classify land coordination failures without implementation reruns' AC1 covers land/ref/index/evidence-staging failure; child 'Mark decomposed parent beads execution-ineligible before retry' AC1 covers decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads; child 'Classify repeated pre-claim system warnings as operator-visible worker state' AC1 covers repeated pre-claim system-unready warning."},{"parent_ac":"2. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_EmptyReviewResultCannotClose` proves a non-skipped success path with `cycle_trace.review_result={}` or an equivalent empty `ReviewResult` cannot close a bead and records `review_error` or operator-attention with a non-empty reason.","coverage":"Child 'Enforce execute-bead review evidence before success close' AC1."},{"parent_ac":"3. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_NilReviewerRequiresExplicitSkip` updates the current nil-reviewer behavior so `Reviewer: nil` only closes when an explicit durable no-review/skip reason is present; otherwise it fails review gating.","coverage":"Child 'Enforce execute-bead review evidence before success close' AC2."},{"parent_ac":"4. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.","coverage":"Child 'Classify land coordination failures without implementation reruns' AC1 and AC2."},{"parent_ac":"5. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_DecomposedParentIsExecutionIneligible` simulates `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`; it asserts the parent is marked execution-ineligible/proposed or dependency-waiting according to policy and that no further implementation attempt or min-power escalation is scheduled.","coverage":"Child 'Mark decomposed parent beads execution-ineligible before retry' AC1 and AC2."},{"parent_ac":"6. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile. Land coordination, repeated pre-claim system warnings, decomposed-parent outcomes, and dirty generated DDx evidence must not raise min power.","coverage":"Child 'Classify repeated pre-claim system warnings as operator-visible worker state' AC2 carries the full cross-scenario test; child 'Classify land coordination failures without implementation reruns' AC2 covers land coordination; child 'Mark decomposed parent beads execution-ineligible before retry' AC2 covers decomposed parent."},{"parent_ac":"7. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, review status/verdict or explicit skip reason, and land/reconcile status for the Niflheim scenario families.","coverage":"Child 'Enforce execute-bead review evidence before success close' AC3 covers review audit; child 'Classify land coordination failures without implementation reruns' AC3 covers land/reconcile audit; child 'Mark decomposed parent beads execution-ineligible before retry' AC3 covers decomposed-parent audit; child 'Classify repeated pre-claim system warnings as operator-visible worker state' AC3 covers pre-claim audit."},{"parent_ac":"8. Existing related behavior from `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, and `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered; this bead either reuses their helpers or waits for those dependencies, and does not add duplicate low-level implementations.","coverage":"Child 'Classify land coordination failures without implementation reruns' AC4 preserves `ddx-2965925e` and `ddx-b2846610`; child 'Classify repeated pre-claim system warnings as operator-visible worker state' AC4 preserves `ddx-d67f124e`; every child description preserves all three dependency IDs and the parent non-scope constraints."},{"parent_ac":"9. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|EmptyReviewResult|NilReviewer|LandCoordination|DecomposedParent|RetryEscalation|CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.","coverage":"Covered as child-specific focused gates: review child AC4, land child AC5, decomposed-parent child AC5, pre-claim/escalation child AC5."},{"parent_ac":"10. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.","coverage":"Review child AC5, land child AC6, decomposed-parent child AC6, pre-claim/escalation child AC6."},{"parent_ac":"11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","coverage":"Review child AC6, land child AC7, decomposed-parent child AC7, pre-claim/escalation child AC7."}],"child_ids":["ddx-cdd1a0b2","ddx-bced4e39","ddx-d2725150","ddx-eea2dfca"],"rationale":"The parent bead mixes four independently testable failure families: review evidence gating, land coordination classification, decomposed-parent execution eligibility, and repeated pre-claim system warnings/retry escalation. Splitting on those boundaries keeps each child within one worker decision path while preserving the same governing references, parent non-scope constraints, dependency IDs, Niflheim incident facts, audit-field requirements, go test gates, and pre-commit gate. The only deliberately cross-scenario assertion is the min-power escalation rule, assigned to the pre-claim/escalation child with supporting focused assertions in the land and decomposed-parent children so no child needs to depend on another child."}
summary: decomposed into ddx-cdd1a0b2, ddx-bced4e39, ddx-d2725150, ddx-eea2dfca
closed as dead-intermediate bead (all children closed, execution-eligible=false)
